background: Homeworld: Kukulkan (Solomani Rim 1925) - Grew up in working-class orbital stations, son of dock workers. Marines were his way out and up. Became one of elite 'Rim Raiders' special operations units. SolSec recruited him after particularly successful covert operation during post-Rim War instability. Combat veteran with 4 terms active service, decorated multiple times.
terms: Marine: Gun Combat (slug)-1, Melee (blade)-1, Athletics (strength)-1. Basic training and first deployment. Advanced to rank 1.
Marine: Gun Combat (slug)-2, Athletics (dexterity)-1, Recon-1. Combat operations, beginning to excel. Advanced to rank 2.
Marine: Gun Combat (slug)-3, Gun Combat (energy)-1, Tactics (military)-1, Stealth-1. Joined 'Rim Raiders' special operations. Advanced to rank 3.
Marine: Melee (blade)-2, Athletics (strength)-2, Athletics (dexterity)-2, Explosives-1. Elite special operations, multiple decorations. Advanced to rank 4. Mustering out: Combat Armor.
Agent (Intelligence): Tactics (military)-2, Recon-2, Heavy Weapons-1, Mechanic-1. Recruited by SolSec for tactical expertise. Advanced to rank 1.

Agent (Intelligence): Gun Combat (energy)-2, Stealth-2, Explosives-2, Survival-1, Carouse-1. Team's tactical specialist and security lead. Advanced to rank 2. Mustering out: Gauss Rifle (customized), Accelerator Rifle (sniper), Combat Knife (mono-edge), Demolition Charges, 50000cr.
interactions: Expertise: Combat operations, weapons (all types), explosives, tactical planning, reconnaissance, unconventional warfare, security assessments, threat elimination, zero-G combat, survival
Weaknesses: Chip on shoulder about being 'the muscle' when he's smarter than assumed, working-class background can create social friction in high society, slight distrust of non-humans initially
Role: Security/Tactical - Handles protection, combat operations, security assessments, elimination of threats. Poses as bodyguard, security consultant, or corporate troubleshooter
Special: Athletic physique from lifelong training. Street smart from rough upbringing. Surprisingly well-read from long deployments
